threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait for the bus at the stop instead of checking your phone for live updates. It's like searching for a paper timetable in 2023.
Last week on the 59, a guy serenaded the entire bus with ballads about lost love. Didn't even miss a stop, just kept going.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a kid selling homemade lemonade on the corner by the canal, people stopping for a chat instead of rushing by. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a Späti with a dog-friendly beer garden where the local artist community gathers every Thursday night for live painting sessions.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they casually mention the BART delays while ordering a burrito, like it’s just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 at Civic Center while a saxophonist played John Coltrane. Dogs and techies spilled out, all moving to the same beat.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think our streets are all for strolling, but locals dodge bikes, scooters, and outdoor diners like a game of Tetris.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their constant map-checking at intersections, looking as lost as a tourist in the Gothic quarter.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when someone mentions the 'Book Club' but isn't talking about a reading group. Just wait till you see the dance floor.
Last night on the 196, a guy serenaded the whole bus with classic 90s R&B. The driver didn’t even blink. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home? Hearing a group of friends argue passionately about which Döner is the best while sipping on a Club Mate at Herrnhuter.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you see people drinking cans of Club Mate at 8 AM while lining up for artisan bagels. It's like a morning ritual here.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know the best spots to dodge the BART delays—like popping into that hole-in-the-wall taqueria on 16th instead of waiting at Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 49 yesterday at Civic Center only to see a dog chilling on the tracks. Muni delays are so common, I half-expected it.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume all Catalans are fluent in Spanish, but many in Gràcia prefer to speak Catalan, and it’s part of our identity.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they can't figure out the 'tapa' system — ordering a full dish instead of small plates is a classic rookie move.
